rows.upgrade: Core primitive contract registry (`node/cell/task/capability/object/stream/journal/budget/policy/model/supervisor/attestation`)
rows.why: The architecture needs one canonical primitive surface before additional profile-specific work can be trusted.
rows.exitCriteria: Delivered authoritative registry contract (`planes/contracts/metakernel_primitives_v1.json`) and enforcement runtime (`core/layer0/ops/src/metakernel.rs`) with unknown-primitive usage scanning over `client/runtime/config/**/*.json`; verified via `npm run -s ops:metakernel:registry`.

rows.upgrade: Invariant gate for the first eight metakernel invariants
rows.why: Safety guarantees must be executable and fail-closed, not descriptive text only.
rows.exitCriteria: Delivered `ops:metakernel:invariants` lane (`protheus-ops metakernel invariants --strict=1`) evaluating all 8 metakernel invariants with deterministic receipts; verified via `npm run -s ops:metakernel:invariants`.

rows.upgrade: `CellBundle` manifest schema + validator lane
rows.why: Portable deploy units need a strict manifest contract for capability/budget/provenance correctness.
rows.exitCriteria: Delivered strict CellBundle schema + example (`planes/contracts/cellbundle.schema.json`, `planes/contracts/examples/cellbundle.minimal.json`) and strict manifest validator (`protheus-ops metakernel manifest --strict=1`); verified via `npm run -s ops:metakernel:manifest`.

rows.upgrade: WIT world registry + wasm component ABI contract
rows.why: Pluggable substrate execution requires a stable component boundary independent of TS wrappers.
rows.exitCriteria: Delivered world registry contract (`planes/contracts/wit/world_registry_v1.json`) and compatibility validation lane (`protheus-ops metakernel worlds --strict=1`) that validates manifest-declared worlds/capabilities against registry; verified via `npm run -s lane:v7-meta-004:run`.

rows.upgrade: Effect-typed capability taxonomy + risk class policy defaults (R0-R4)
rows.why: Capability safety depends on typed effects and deterministic risk-tier handling rules.
rows.exitCriteria: Delivered capability taxonomy policy (`planes/contracts/capability_effect_taxonomy_v1.json`) and strict gating lane (`protheus-ops metakernel capability-taxonomy --strict=1`) for effect/risk derivation with high-risk policy-gate checks + receipts; verified via `npm run -s lane:v7-meta-005:run`.

rows.upgrade: Budget admission controller across CPU/RAM/storage/network/tokens/power/privacy/cognitive-load
rows.why: Budgets must bind before execution to prevent post-hoc overrun behavior.
rows.exitCriteria: Delivered admission policy (`planes/contracts/budget_admission_policy_v1.json`) and fail-closed admission lane (`protheus-ops metakernel budget-admission --strict=1`) that emits bounded `reason_codes` on denial; verified via `npm run -s lane:v7-meta-006:run`.

rows.upgrade: Epistemic object schema v1 (value/schema/provenance/confidence/policy/retention/export/rollback)
rows.why: AI-native state needs typed uncertainty and lineage metadata, not byte-only storage semantics.
rows.exitCriteria: Delivered `planes/contracts/epistemic_object_v1.schema.json` + example object and strict metakernel validator lane (`protheus-ops metakernel epistemic-object --strict=1`); verified via `npm run -s lane:v7-meta-007:run`.

rows.upgrade: Effect journal commit-before-actuate enforcement
rows.why: External side effects must be journaled deterministically to preserve replay and audit guarantees.
rows.exitCriteria: Delivered effect-journal policy + example (`planes/contracts/effect_journal_policy_v1.json`, `planes/contracts/examples/effect_journal.minimal.json`) and strict enforcement lane (`protheus-ops metakernel effect-journal --strict=1`); verified via `npm run -s lane:v7-meta-008:run`.

rows.upgrade: Substrate descriptor registry + degrade matrix
rows.why: Portable execution across MCU/edge/cloud/quantum/neural nodes requires machine-readable substrate contracts.
rows.exitCriteria: Delivered substrate descriptor registry (`planes/contracts/substrate_descriptor_registry_v1.json`) with required descriptor fields + degrade matrix checks and strict validator lane; verified via `npm run -s lane:v7-meta-009:run`.

rows.upgrade: Radix policy guard (binary baseline, ternary optional class-scoped lowering)
rows.why: Radix experimentation must not weaken core security invariants.
rows.exitCriteria: Delivered radix guard policy (`planes/contracts/radix_policy_guard_v1.json`) and strict guard lane (`protheus-ops metakernel radix-guard --strict=1`) enforcing binary-required critical paths + ternary overlap rejection; verified via `npm run -s lane:v7-meta-010:run`.

rows.upgrade: Quantum broker domain contract (`compile/estimate/submit/session/batch/measure`)
rows.why: Quantum execution is hybrid and queue-bound; it needs explicit broker semantics and fallback guarantees.
rows.exitCriteria: Delivered `planes/contracts/quantum_broker_domain_v1.json` and strict lane `protheus-ops metakernel quantum-broker --strict=1` validating required operations + classical fallback receipt policy; verified via `npm run -s lane:v7-meta-011:run`.

rows.upgrade: Neural consent kernel scaffold with separated authorities (`observe/infer/feedback/stimulate`)
rows.why: Neural interfaces require strict authority boundaries and highest-risk controls for stimulation paths.
rows.exitCriteria: Delivered `planes/contracts/neural_consent_kernel_v1.json` and strict lane `protheus-ops metakernel neural-consent --strict=1` enforcing separated authorities and dual-control/consent/audit stimulate policy; verified via `npm run -s lane:v7-meta-012:run`.

rows.upgrade: Attestation graph service for code/model/policy/data/effect lineage
rows.why: Runtime trust requires signed lineage across artifacts, not isolated per-lane proofs.
rows.exitCriteria: Delivered `planes/contracts/attestation_graph_v1.json` and strict lane `protheus-ops metakernel attestation-graph --strict=1` validating domain linkage across code/model/policy/data/effect edges; verified via `npm run -s lane:v7-meta-013:run`.

rows.upgrade: Degradation-contract verifier (`no-network/no-ternary/no-qpu/neural-link-loss`)
rows.why: Degraded operation must preserve safety while staying operational within declared fallbacks.
rows.exitCriteria: Delivered `planes/contracts/degradation_contracts_v1.json` and strict lane `protheus-ops metakernel degradation-contracts --strict=1` enforcing per-critical-lane fallback declarations with no privilege widening; verified via `npm run -s lane:v7-meta-014:run`.

rows.upgrade: Execution profile matrix harness (`mcu`, `edge`, `cloud`)
rows.why: Profile claims need reproducible execution evidence, not architecture-only assertions.
rows.exitCriteria: Delivered `planes/contracts/execution_profile_matrix_v1.json` and strict lane `protheus-ops metakernel execution-profiles --strict=1` verifying required profile IDs and harness declarations; verified via `npm run -s lane:v7-meta-015:run`.

rows.upgrade: Layer-0 machine-checked invariant proof pack (Lean/Coq/Rocq)
rows.why: Invariants are documented and partially enforced, but not fully machine-checked end-to-end as a single trust artifact.
rows.exitCriteria: Prove Layer-0 invariants (conduit-only path, constitution hardening + Merkle/operator approval, receipt state binding/anti-forgery, fail-closed boundaries) in a machine-checked proof bundle with deterministic proof artifacts.

rows.upgrade: Core Rust formal proof coverage lane (Kani/Prusti)
rows.why: Runtime hardening still relies heavily on tests/guards rather than broad property proofs across core crates.
rows.exitCriteria: Add Kani/Prusti proof lanes for core crates with required pass receipts and explicit coverage map for proven vs unproven surfaces.

rows.upgrade: Nightly full-kernel re-proof CI + `verify.sh` proof gate
rows.why: Proof posture can drift without continuous re-verification across new commits.
rows.exitCriteria: Extend CI with nightly proof re-run gate and bind `verify.sh` to proof/artifact verification so proof drift fails closed.

rows.upgrade: Reproducible public proof VM artifact lane
rows.why: External trust requires independent replayability of proof verification, not internal-only receipts.
rows.exitCriteria: Publish reproducible VM/container artifact that replays proof verification deterministically and emits signed verification receipts.

rows.upgrade: Single static `protheusd` binary profile (25–35MB target)
rows.why: Runtime still depends on distributed wrappers/host assumptions that weaken deployment simplicity and assurance claims.
rows.exitCriteria: Deliver static single-binary `protheusd` profile (musl + cargo dist/Nix path) with zero runtime external dependency requirement in default operator mode.

rows.upgrade: Single-binary reproducible supply-chain release contract
rows.why: Production-depth deployment needs deterministic artifact lineage and independent reproducibility.
rows.exitCriteria: Build single-binary releases with reproducible build metadata, SBOM, signatures, and provenance receipts; CI verifies reproducibility thresholds before release promotion.

rows.upgrade: Public benchmark harness with strict performance thresholds
rows.why: Comparative claims need reproducible, policy-bound performance evidence.
rows.exitCriteria: Publish deterministic benchmark harness and CI artifacts proving target thresholds (cold-start <=80ms, idle <=25MB RSS, >=5k tasks/sec) with regression gates.

rows.upgrade: Public “Protheus vs X” CI-updated comparison matrix
rows.why: Comparative positioning drifts without continuously regenerated side-by-side evidence.
rows.exitCriteria: Add CI-updated comparison table from canonical benchmark artifacts with deterministic generation script and evidence links.

rows.upgrade: Independent third-party verification publication lane
rows.why: External assurance requires non-self-asserted verification from respected third parties.
rows.exitCriteria: Human-contracted external verification is completed and published with linked findings/remediation evidence (`HMAN-087`).

rows.upgrade: High-assurance profile release gate (medical/defense-class)
rows.why: High-stakes profile claims require governance/legal scope approval beyond technical implementation.
rows.exitCriteria: Human-approved high-assurance profile scope and claim boundaries are published, and profile artifacts pass policy + proof + benchmark gates (`HMAN-086`).

rows.upgrade: Layer `-1` variant-profile manifest system (medical/robotics/ai-isolation/riscv-sovereign presets)
rows.why: The report calls out domain-hardened variants as a key leverage point not yet represented as one canonical profile contract.
rows.exitCriteria: Add variant profile manifests with deterministic capability/budget/policy deltas and validation lanes; each variant emits profile receipts proving no privilege widening versus baseline safety policy.

rows.upgrade: Embedded MPU compartment profile contract
rows.why: Embedded isolation claims require explicit MPU compartment semantics instead of generic substrate claims.
rows.exitCriteria: Add MPU profile descriptors + conformance checks for compartment boundaries and failure isolation, with deterministic pass/fail receipts for embedded targets.

rows.upgrade: Capability grant/revoke hash-chain ledger
rows.why: Capability mutations need immutable per-event chaining beyond aggregate receipt streams to improve forensic trust.
rows.exitCriteria: Every capability grant/revoke appends to a deterministic hash chain (id, actor, scope, previous_hash); verifier rejects tampered or missing links.

rows.upgrade: Dual-metered WASM sandbox policy (fuel + epoch) as default tool-exec envelope
rows.why: Tool execution isolation is stronger when compute ceilings are enforced by two orthogonal meters.
rows.exitCriteria: Add dual-meter policy and runtime enforcement so sandbox executions fail-closed on either fuel or epoch exhaustion, with meter telemetry in receipts.

rows.upgrade: Persistent `Hands` capability-pack runtime (`HAND.toml`)
rows.why: Scheduled autonomous capability packs are currently fragmented across lanes and need one reusable primitive.
rows.exitCriteria: Add manifest-driven `Hands` runtime with scheduled execution, capability scoping, and deterministic lifecycle receipts (`install/start/pause/rotate`).

rows.upgrade: ABAC policy plane + flight-recorder trace contract
rows.why: Role-only controls are insufficient for richer enterprise policy decisions and replay-grade auditability.
rows.exitCriteria: Introduce ABAC evaluator (subject/object/action/context) with immutable flight-recorder traces for allow/deny decisions and bounded redaction policy.

rows.upgrade: Local-first CRDT object-store adapter lane (Automerge profile)
rows.why: Sovereign/offline-first memory continuity needs a concrete CRDT adapter contract, not only conceptual references.
rows.exitCriteria: Ship CRDT adapter lane with deterministic merge/replay tests, export/reconstruct guarantees, and conflict provenance receipts.

rows.upgrade: Trust-chain integration lane (reproducible build + Sigstore/Rekor + measured-boot pointers)
rows.why: Trust controls are present in pieces; this intake needs one integrated executable chain for build-to-runtime trust evidence.
rows.exitCriteria: Build artifacts include reproducibility metadata + signature/log proofs + measured-boot pointer linkage, all verifiable via one deterministic trust-chain command.

rows.upgrade: Proof-preserving fastpath optimization lane for Layer-2 hotpaths
rows.why: Performance can improve without weakening assurances only if fastpaths are explicitly constrained and verified.
rows.exitCriteria: Add bounded fastpaths for selected Layer-2 hotpaths with property checks proving parity to canonical path and automatic rollback on parity failure.

rows.upgrade: Industrial process mapping pack (ISA-95/RAMI 4.0 templates)
rows.why: Enterprise industrial adoption needs concrete process-mapping artifacts, not generic architecture claims.
rows.exitCriteria: Add templates/examples that map InfRing primitives to ISA-95/RAMI concepts with validation checklist and documentation links.

rows.upgrade: Multi-level stealth fetcher engine
rows.why: Current research/assimilate flows are fragile on anti-bot protected targets and do not adapt fetch strategy by protection level.
rows.exitCriteria: Add fast HTTP + stealth fetcher + dynamic browser fetch modes with automatic selector based on protection signals; protected target scrape runs show fallback switching and bounded detection failures.

rows.upgrade: Adaptive element tracking and selector recovery
rows.why: Site layout churn breaks brittle selectors and causes repeated manual parser maintenance.
rows.exitCriteria: Implement adaptive element recovery (CSS/XPath/text/regex + similarity fallback) so parser contracts survive representative site redesign fixtures without manual selector edits.

rows.upgrade: Smart scaling + resilience lane for crawling
rows.why: Large crawl jobs need deterministic queueing/retry/checkpoint behavior to avoid partial-loss failures.
rows.exitCriteria: Ship concurrent crawler controls with per-domain throttle, checkpoint pause/resume, and exponential backoff retries; prove 100+ concurrent request profile with graceful recovery receipts.

rows.upgrade: MCP extraction integration for pre-structured research payloads
rows.why: LLM burn remains high when raw pages are forwarded without pre-extraction contracts.
rows.exitCriteria: Provide MCP-callable research endpoint that returns structured pre-extracted artifacts (content + metadata + provenance receipts) prior to model invocation.

rows.upgrade: Safety-plane routed stealth research execution + deterministic receipts
rows.why: Research tooling must not bypass conduit/policy enforcement even when running stealth/browser fetch paths.
rows.exitCriteria: Enforce research execution through Layer-0 conduit gate with policy/budget checks and deterministic per-action receipts; no direct bypass paths from client wrappers.

rows.upgrade: Developer-facing stealth research DX (`protheus research --stealth`)
rows.why: Adoption friction remains high without one-command CLI/API defaults and thin-wrapper conventions.
rows.exitCriteria: Add thin client wrappers and CLI flags for stealth mode (`protheus research --stealth`, `protheus assimilate --stealth`) with zero-config defaults and operator-visible diagnostics.

rows.upgrade: Rule-Based CrawlSpider + LinkExtractor Contract
rows.why: Current crawling lanes lack a canonical rule-driven traversal engine for large structured crawls, causing repeated bespoke crawl logic.
rows.exitCriteria: Add governed spider class contract supporting allow/deny link rules, callback routing, depth/domain constraints, and deterministic per-link traversal receipts; `protheus crawl spider <name> --rules <config>` executes rule-based crawls over the same conduit-governed research pipeline.

rows.exitCriteria: Add nightly self-hosted full-proof farm lane (Lean/Kani/TLA+/chaos/bench regressions), artifact retention policy, and annual substrate-proof refresh checklist with drift alerts.
rows.section: Infallible Origin Master Plan Intake (Google Doc `1QuwfA-EGZA4kCqfzrvFaeahszRskJLg7Moc92u4m2U0`, 2026-03-08)

rows.upgrade: Local Public APIs Catalog Snapshot + Refresh Lane
rows.why: Research/assimilate flows do not have one governed local catalog of public APIs, causing repeated ad hoc source discovery and integration drift.
rows.exitCriteria: Add local catalog ingestion lane with deterministic schema, category indexing, and scheduled refresh (`daily/weekly`) receipts; `protheus api search` can query local catalog without network dependency once hydrated.

rows.upgrade: API Discovery + Recommendation Engine
rows.why: Natural-language API discovery is currently manual and does not rank options by task relevance, policy fit, or operational constraints.
rows.exitCriteria: Add discovery lane that parses NL intent, ranks candidate APIs by relevance + reliability metadata, and emits policy-aware recommendations with explicit rate-limit/auth notes and deterministic receipts.

rows.upgrade: One-Click API Wrapper Generation Contract
rows.why: API onboarding is too manual; wrappers are inconsistent and can bypass conduit contracts if hand-authored ad hoc.
rows.exitCriteria: Add generator lane for Rust core integration stubs + thin TS client wrappers with conduit routing, schema validation, and receipt hooks; generated wrappers pass scaffold contract tests.

rows.upgrade: Conduit-Only API Call Enforcement
rows.why: External API calls risk bypassing Safety Plane constraints when routed directly from client-side code paths.
rows.exitCriteria: Enforce all generated and runtime API calls through conduit with capability/budget checks and deterministic receipts; contract checker fails builds on direct bypass patterns.

rows.upgrade: Developer Surface (`api search` / `api integrate`) + Observability View
rows.why: Developer ergonomics for discovering/integrating APIs remain fragmented and under-instrumented.
rows.exitCriteria: Provide CLI surfaces (`protheus api search`, `protheus api integrate`) and observability panel summary in `protheus-top` with integration status, last refresh age, and receipt-linked call metrics.

rows.upgrade: Decentralized Data Discovery Catalog
rows.why: Dataset discovery is fragmented across isolated sources and nodes, limiting RSI network utility and repeatability for research ingestion.
rows.exitCriteria: Add local + P2P searchable dataset catalog with periodic sync from connected nodes/external open sources; `protheus data search "<query>"` returns ranked results with provenance metadata.
rows.section: Decentralized Open Data Marketplace Intake (Source bundle: Ocean/OriginTrail/data.all/Opendatabay/Delta Sharing, 2026-03-09)

rows.upgrade: Secure Data Sharing with Provenance Contracts
rows.why: Data exchange currently lacks one uniform capability-gated sharing contract with cryptographic provenance and retention policy controls.
rows.exitCriteria: Implement capability/tokenized sharing envelopes where each shared dataset carries Epistemic metadata (provenance/confidence/policy/retention) and produces a deterministic receipt chain; raw transfer denied without explicit grant.
rows.section: Decentralized Open Data Marketplace Intake (Source bundle: Ocean/OriginTrail/data.all/Opendatabay/Delta Sharing, 2026-03-09)

rows.upgrade: Compute-to-Data Execution Lane
rows.why: Current workflows often require copying data to compute, increasing exposure and policy risk for sensitive datasets.
rows.exitCriteria: Add remote compute-to-data execution so analysis runs at data owner node and returns bounded result artifacts only; raw dataset remains local and policy-enforced receipts prove execution path.
rows.section: Decentralized Open Data Marketplace Intake (Source bundle: Ocean/OriginTrail/data.all/Opendatabay/Delta Sharing, 2026-03-09)

rows.upgrade: Knowledge Graph Provenance Index Integration
rows.why: Shared datasets are not consistently indexed with lineage relationships, reducing traceability and downstream recall quality.
rows.exitCriteria: Integrate marketplace datasets into knowledge graph index with lineage edges and provenance links; search/assimilation outputs include graph relationships and source ancestry.
rows.section: Decentralized Open Data Marketplace Intake (Source bundle: Ocean/OriginTrail/data.all/Opendatabay/Delta Sharing, 2026-03-09)

rows.upgrade: Conduit-Only Marketplace Enforcement + Receipts
rows.why: Discovery/sharing/compute marketplace actions can bypass canonical audit flow without explicit conduit gating.
rows.exitCriteria: Enforce conduit-only routing for all marketplace operations with deterministic receipts, capability checks, and policy/budget gates; contract checker fails on bypass paths.
rows.section: Decentralized Open Data Marketplace Intake (Source bundle: Ocean/OriginTrail/data.all/Opendatabay/Delta Sharing, 2026-03-09)

rows.upgrade: Structured Report -> Issue -> Session -> PR Pipeline Contract
rows.why: Distributed improvement loops stay manual and inconsistent without one deterministic progression from observation to merge candidate.
rows.exitCriteria: Implement governed pipeline where any valid report object can promote through issue/session/PR stages with explicit state transitions, deterministic receipts, and policy-gated stage advancement.

rows.upgrade: Cross-Fork Convergence Detection Contract

rows.upgrade: Fork Sovereignty Lifecycle Contract

rows.exitCriteria: Define lifecycle operations (`create/register/track/diverge/archive`) bound to sovereign identity objects, with deterministic health/divergence receipts and fail-closed policy on unauthorized fork operations.
rows.section: Decentralized Fork Convergence & Structured Feedback Pipeline Source Coverage Intake (Doc `1b2fSTI35W4dbOzJ5ZWAWw15dWGf-P_X9TuvgqL0qhhk`, 2026-03-11)

rows.upgrade: Consent-Gated Multi-Tier Review Contract
rows.why: Autonomous merge loops are unsafe without explicit fast/deep review tiers and human consent control for high-impact changes.
rows.exitCriteria: Require two-tier review (`fast-pass`, `deep-arch`) with policy thresholds for auto-advance vs human approval, and deterministic review decision receipts linked to each PR candidate.

rows.upgrade: Dogfooding Improvement Loop Contract
rows.why: Infrastructure quality stalls when agents do not continuously exercise and validate their own improvement machinery in production-like flows.
rows.exitCriteria: Add governed dogfooding mode where orchestration, convergence, and review paths run against self-hosted infrastructure changes with failure routing, rollback hooks, and observability evidence artifacts.

rows.upgrade: Conduit-Only Network Convergence Boundary + Thin-Surface Enforcement
rows.why: Fork/convergence orchestration can bypass safety policy if implemented in client/app logic rather than authoritative core lanes.
rows.exitCriteria: Enforce conduit-only execution for all report/fork/convergence/review actions, reject bypass paths in contract checks, and keep client/app layers strictly non-authoritative UX/control surfaces.

rows.upgrade: Automated Script Generation Lane

rows.upgrade: UGC-Style Video Generation Lane

rows.upgrade: High-Volume Ad Factory Batch Mode

rows.upgrade: Conduit-Only Ad Generation Enforcement

rows.upgrade: `/apps` Default App Placement + Persona Hooks

rows.upgrade: Autonomous Training Loop Engine

rows.upgrade: Dual Iteration Mode (Prompt Agent + Code Agent)

rows.upgrade: Git-Based Experiment Tracking + Merge Policy

rows.upgrade: Hardware-Agnostic Single-GPU Runtime Profile

rows.exitCriteria: Provide single-GPU profile with budget-aware scheduling, deterministic fallback behavior, and no required external managed training service for baseline operation.
rows.section: Autoresearch Self-Improving Loop Intake (Source: Karpathy `autoresearch` pattern + Alex Finn thread, 2026-03-09)

rows.upgrade: Conduit-Only Self-Improvement Enforcement + Receipts
rows.why: Training and git mutation paths can bypass canonical safety/policy controls if executed directly from tool/app layers.
rows.exitCriteria: Route all training runs, git mutations, and model promotions through conduit with capability/budget checks and deterministic receipts; bypass detections fail closed via contract gates.
rows.section: Autoresearch Self-Improving Loop Intake (Source: Karpathy `autoresearch` pattern + Alex Finn thread, 2026-03-09)

rows.upgrade: Conversation correction/reward capture extension
rows.why: RL-style improvement needs governed reward signals from real usage, but conversation feedback/corrections are not yet modeled as a canonical training signal contract.
rows.exitCriteria: Add reward-capture primitives that convert bounded user corrections/approvals/failures into redacted reward objects with provenance, privacy policy labels, and deterministic receipts; routine turns can be scored as `positive/negative/neutral` or token/span-level corrections without leaking raw sensitive content into training lanes.
rows.section: Autoresearch Self-Improving Loop Intake (Source: Karpathy `autoresearch` pattern + Alex Finn thread, 2026-03-09)

rows.upgrade: Async train/serve coexistence + hot-swap promotion contract
rows.why: Background training only helps if serving remains stable and improved candidates can be promoted without interrupting live operation or widening failure blast radius.
rows.exitCriteria: Add async serving/training coexistence contract where candidate policies train in parallel, pass eval/policy gates, and promote through bounded hot-swap/canary/rollback flow with no serving interruption and deterministic promotion receipts.
rows.section: Autoresearch Self-Improving Loop Intake (Source: Karpathy `autoresearch` pattern + Alex Finn thread, 2026-03-09)

rows.upgrade: Hard-case targeting policy for policy-level failures
rows.why: Self-improvement loops waste budget when they spend training cycles on memory/retrieval/skill failures that should be fixed elsewhere.
rows.exitCriteria: Add targeting policy that routes only policy-level failures (planning, tool ordering, ambiguity handling, sequencing) into RL/distillation queues while explicitly excluding memory/index/skill/config failures unless reclassified; targeting decisions emit deterministic receipts and budget telemetry.
rows.section: Autoresearch Self-Improving Loop Intake (Source: Karpathy `autoresearch` pattern + Alex Finn thread, 2026-03-09)

rows.upgrade: Decoupled Async RL Loop Topology Contract (Serve/Rollout/Judge/Train)
rows.why: Background policy learning remains fragile without a formal decoupled topology for serving, rollout collection, reward judging, and training loops.
rows.exitCriteria: Add governed four-loop async topology contract with bounded queue interfaces between loops, deterministic loop-state receipts, and non-blocking serve guarantees during training and promotion windows.
rows.section: Autoresearch Self-Improving Loop Intake (Source: Karpathy `autoresearch` pattern + Alex Finn thread, 2026-03-09)

rows.upgrade: Policy Objective Mode Contract (Binary Reward vs Token-Distillation)
rows.why: RL pipelines need explicit objective selection to avoid ad hoc switching between reward-style and distillation-style learning runs.

rows.section: Autoresearch Self-Improving Loop Intake (Source: Karpathy `autoresearch` pattern + Alex Finn thread, 2026-03-09)

rows.upgrade: Wake-Up Loop + Self-Re-Trigger Contract

rows.upgrade: Persona-aware opt-in activation + observability surfacing
rows.why: Default Eyes/Hands must remain opt-in and visible, otherwise packaged capability silently widens the runtime surface.
rows.exitCriteria: Extend persona bindings and observability so relevant packaged Eyes/Hands can be suggested or enabled per persona with explicit opt-in, clear status in `protheus-top`, and deterministic activation/telemetry receipts.
rows.section: Default Eyes & Hands Framework Intake (Internal design doc, 2026-03-11)

rows.upgrade: Conduit-only Eye/Hand guardrails + adapter boundaries
rows.why: Packaged defaults increase attack surface unless all sensing/action I/O remains policy-gated and adapter-specific logic is kept outside client/app authority.
rows.exitCriteria: Enforce Layer-0 conduit checks for all Eye/Hand I/O, keep provider/device specifics in adapters, and emit deterministic receipts for registry loads, activations, polls, tool invocations, and policy denials.
rows.section: Default Eyes & Hands Framework Intake (Internal design doc, 2026-03-11)

rows.upgrade: Specialization + paid-task discovery app workflow
rows.why: Autonomous economic agents need a reusable workflow for specialization-constrained task discovery, but this should compose existing research/marketplace primitives rather than create a bespoke core subsystem.
rows.exitCriteria: Ship `/apps` workflow that lets an agent adopt a specialization profile and discover paid tasks from approved marketplaces/providers using existing discovery primitives, with deterministic discovery and match receipts.
rows.section: Autonomous Economic Specialization App Intake (CashClaw pattern, 2026-03-11)

rows.upgrade: Feedback/reputation/payment compounding loop over existing primitives
rows.why: Economic specialization only becomes self-improving when payment outcomes, feedback, and learned rules are retained and routed back into memory and policy-improvement lanes.
rows.exitCriteria: Store payments, reputation changes, feedback, and learned specialization rules through existing Epistemic Object/economy primitives, and wire them into governed improvement loops so performance compounds without introducing a separate learning subsystem.
rows.section: Autonomous Economic Specialization App Intake (CashClaw pattern, 2026-03-11)

rows.upgrade: Escrow/payment/reputation handling over economy/ledger primitives
rows.why: Money-making workflows need payment and reputation state, but that state should extend existing economy/ledger primitives instead of inventing a second commerce backend.
rows.exitCriteria: Add app-level payment/reputation workflow that routes escrow simulation, payment receipts, payout confirmation, and reputation updates through existing economy/ledger policies and emits deterministic lifecycle receipts.
rows.section: Autonomous Economic Specialization App Intake (CashClaw pattern, 2026-03-11)

rows.upgrade: Conduit-only economic workflow enforcement + `/apps` placement contract
rows.why: Economic automation increases risk and should remain explicitly outside the client boundary while still using canonical safety and audit paths.
rows.exitCriteria: Ship the specialization workflow under `/apps`, keep any client wrapper thin, and enforce conduit-only routing for discovery, delivery, payment, reputation, and improvement actions with deterministic receipts and fail-closed policy checks.
rows.section: Autonomous Economic Specialization App Intake (CashClaw pattern, 2026-03-11)

rows.upgrade: `CLAUDE.md` Config Parser over Constitution + Rule Objects
rows.why: Coding workflow rules are scattered across prompts and ad hoc notes, so behavior is not consistently reproducible across projects.
rows.exitCriteria: Dropping `CLAUDE.md` in a project root is detected by the app and parsed into governed rule objects (Epistemic Objects) using existing constitution/persona contracts, with deterministic parse/apply receipts and no duplicate rule engine outside core primitives.

rows.upgrade: Planning-Mode + Sub-Agent Delegation Profile
rows.why: Complex tasks still rely on manual decomposition, causing context bloat and inconsistent delegation behavior.
rows.exitCriteria: The app activates an existing planning/delegation profile for complex tasks, spawning bounded sub-agents via current Layer-2 orchestrator lanes, preserving parent-child lineage and deterministic handoff receipts.

rows.upgrade: Correction-to-Rule Capture Loop
rows.why: User corrections are often lost after a session, so coding behavior does not compound reliably.
rows.exitCriteria: User corrections are converted into policy-scoped rule objects through existing memory/learning primitives with confidence, provenance, and retention metadata, and subsequent runs show rule reuse with deterministic capture/apply receipts.

rows.upgrade: Verification + Autonomous Bug-Fix Execution Profile
rows.why: Code changes can still be committed without a full trace-debug-fix verification loop, increasing regression risk.
rows.exitCriteria: The app binds existing research/verify/debug/fix primitives into a required pre-commit profile that executes tests/checks, performs bounded repair attempts, and blocks blind commits unless policy exceptions are explicitly granted and receipted.

rows.upgrade: Conduit-Only Workflow Enforcement + `/apps` Placement Contract
rows.why: Workflow loaders can drift into client-owned authority paths if placement and guardrails are not explicit.
rows.exitCriteria: Ship implementation under `/apps/claude_workflow/`, keep client wrappers thin (`run/status/explain`), and enforce conduit-only routing for parsing, planning, delegation, verification, and fix actions with deterministic receipts and fail-closed policy denials.

rows.upgrade: Modular Micro-Prompt Registry + Conditional Composition Contract
rows.why: Monolithic persona prompts are hard to evolve safely and can cause drift when small rule changes require full prompt rewrites.
rows.exitCriteria: Add versioned micro-prompt registry with signature/provenance metadata and dynamic composition rules (task/env/sub-agent class), producing deterministic composition receipts and reproducible prompt manifests per run.
rows.section: Claude Code System Prompt Architecture Source Coverage Intake (Doc `1kEEEjVq_1oAVsJt87IveSu-Y7B97JlBowl_P9E3iQ04`, 2026-03-11)

rows.upgrade: Verification-Specialist Sub-Agent Contract
rows.why: Code/task execution quality varies without a mandatory adversarial verification pass that can independently assert PASS/FAIL/PARTIAL outcomes.
rows.exitCriteria: Add governed verification-specialist profile that runs post-execution checks (build/lint/tests/edge probes), emits verdict objects with evidence references, and can gate promotion/commit workflows through policy-defined thresholds.
rows.section: Claude Code System Prompt Architecture Source Coverage Intake (Doc `1kEEEjVq_1oAVsJt87IveSu-Y7B97JlBowl_P9E3iQ04`, 2026-03-11)

rows.upgrade: Persistent Security-Monitor Sub-Agent Contract
rows.why: Tool calls and edits can bypass intent-safety expectations unless a dedicated monitor continuously evaluates action intent/risk against block/allow policy.
rows.exitCriteria: Add always-on security-monitor profile that inspects proposed actions in parallel, issues allow/deny recommendations with deterministic reason codes, and routes final high-risk decisions through Layer-0 deny/approve enforcement receipts.
rows.section: Claude Code System Prompt Architecture Source Coverage Intake (Doc `1kEEEjVq_1oAVsJt87IveSu-Y7B97JlBowl_P9E3iQ04`, 2026-03-11)

rows.upgrade: Project Context File Synthesizer Contract (`CLAUDE.md`/`AGENTS.md` Delta Sync)
rows.why: Project conventions and decisions decay when context files are manually maintained and diverge from actual execution history.
rows.exitCriteria: Add governed context-file synthesis/update contract that proposes or applies bounded diffs for project context docs from receipt-backed history, with policy-scoped write permissions and deterministic change receipts linked to source evidence.
rows.section: Claude Code System Prompt Architecture Source Coverage Intake (Doc `1kEEEjVq_1oAVsJt87IveSu-Y7B97JlBowl_P9E3iQ04`, 2026-03-11)

rows.upgrade: Tool-Usage Hierarchy + Anti-Abstraction Guardrail Contract
rows.why: Agents overuse shell/quick hacks and create unnecessary files/abstractions when tool-selection and engineering-discipline rules are not enforceable runtime objects.
rows.exitCriteria: Add policy objects enforcing preferred tool order (edit/read/write before shell where applicable), bounded shell use tiers, and guardrails for unnecessary file creation/abstraction churn; violations fail closed or require explicit override receipts.
rows.section: Claude Code System Prompt Architecture Source Coverage Intake (Doc `1kEEEjVq_1oAVsJt87IveSu-Y7B97JlBowl_P9E3iQ04`, 2026-03-11)

rows.upgrade: Conduit-Only Prompt Governance Boundary + Thin Client Enforcement
rows.why: Prompt assembly and specialist-agent routing can drift into client-owned code paths, weakening auditability and policy enforcement.
rows.exitCriteria: Enforce conduit-only routing for prompt compose/select/update and specialist-agent invocation/gating actions, with bypass-rejection tests and client/app surfaces limited to non-authoritative UX wrappers.
rows.section: Claude Code System Prompt Architecture Source Coverage Intake (Doc `1kEEEjVq_1oAVsJt87IveSu-Y7B97JlBowl_P9E3iQ04`, 2026-03-11)

rows.upgrade: Explicit Build/Plan Persona Pair + Instant Mode-Switch Contract
rows.why: Coding sessions need clear, operator-visible safety modes; without a first-class switch contract, read-only planning can drift into unintended mutation paths.
rows.exitCriteria: Add governed `build`/`plan` persona pair with instant switch action (`protheus mode build
rows.section: OpenCode Coding-Agent UX Source Coverage Intake (Doc `1e1cAKPgdtYJNntQX2abA6_fNGvoUNQ4V1fKMrXUWHIs`, 2026-03-11)

rows.upgrade: Terminal-Native Coding Cockpit + Embedded LSP Contract
rows.why: Coding-agent usability degrades when terminal workflows and code intelligence (hover/diagnostics/completions) are split across disconnected tools.
rows.exitCriteria: Add `protheus tui` coding cockpit contract with embedded LSP-backed code-intelligence events routed through gateway authority and deterministically traced; client remains render-only and cannot bypass policy gates for file/tool actions.
rows.section: OpenCode Coding-Agent UX Source Coverage Intake (Doc `1e1cAKPgdtYJNntQX2abA6_fNGvoUNQ4V1fKMrXUWHIs`, 2026-03-11)

rows.upgrade: Unified Multi-Client Coding Surface Parity Contract
rows.why: Remote coding control becomes brittle when TUI/desktop/mobile/IDE clients implement divergent command semantics over separate execution paths.
rows.exitCriteria: Define one gateway command/session contract for coding surfaces so TUI, desktop, mobile, and IDE connectors share identical attach/auth/action semantics with deterministic parity receipts and fail-closed auth scope checks.
rows.section: OpenCode Coding-Agent UX Source Coverage Intake (Doc `1e1cAKPgdtYJNntQX2abA6_fNGvoUNQ4V1fKMrXUWHIs`, 2026-03-11)

rows.upgrade: Per-Agent Routing Override Contract over Existing Zen/Router Primitives
rows.why: Teams need per-agent model routing overrides in coding workflows without creating a second provider-routing subsystem.
rows.exitCriteria: Add per-agent routing profile overlays (cost/latency/quality constraints, provider allowlists) that compile into existing router primitives and emit deterministic route-decision receipts with fallback evidence.
rows.section: OpenCode Coding-Agent UX Source Coverage Intake (Doc `1e1cAKPgdtYJNntQX2abA6_fNGvoUNQ4V1fKMrXUWHIs`, 2026-03-11)

rows.upgrade: OpenCode-Compatible Profile Template Governance Lane
rows.why: Teams migrating from OpenCode need familiar build/plan/TUI presets, but unmanaged imports can bypass policy and drift from core contracts.
rows.exitCriteria: Maintain signed template pack installable via governed URI (for example `opencode://build-plan`) that maps to existing core primitives, with quarterly human review, compatibility metadata, and deterministic install/activate/deprecate receipts.
rows.section: OpenCode Coding-Agent UX Source Coverage Intake (Doc `1e1cAKPgdtYJNntQX2abA6_fNGvoUNQ4V1fKMrXUWHIs`, 2026-03-11)

rows.upgrade: Conduit-Only Coding Cockpit Boundary + Thin Client Enforcement
rows.why: Rich coding UX can accidentally centralize authority in client-side logic if boundary rules are not explicit and tested.
rows.exitCriteria: Enforce conduit-only routing for mode switching, tool/file actions, LSP-triggered operations, and remote client commands; add bypass-rejection tests proving client/app surfaces remain non-authoritative wrappers.
rows.section: OpenCode Coding-Agent UX Source Coverage Intake (Doc `1e1cAKPgdtYJNntQX2abA6_fNGvoUNQ4V1fKMrXUWHIs`, 2026-03-11)

rows.upgrade: Elegant GUI Management Layer
rows.why: Setup and day-2 operations remain CLI-heavy, increasing operator friction for routine maintenance and onboarding.
rows.exitCriteria: `protheus gui` exposes a self-hosted management UI (deploy/bootstrap/status/settings) including env/model-key controls and management receipts for all actions.

rows.upgrade: Auto-Backup + GitHub Sync Lane
rows.why: Workspace/config/state recovery depends on manual backup discipline, risking drift and lost operational context.
rows.exitCriteria: Add policy-gated auto-backup lane with one-click import/sync and deterministic commit/push receipts for backup actions.

rows.upgrade: Prompt Hardening + Drift Doctor Engine
rows.why: Prompt/config drift can silently degrade quality and policy compliance without continuous diagnostic tooling.
rows.exitCriteria: `protheus doctor drift` scans prompts/workspace state, emits hardening recommendations, and writes deterministic drift-analysis receipts.

rows.upgrade: Telegram Multi-Topic Setup Wizard
rows.why: Multi-topic Telegram orchestration is operationally complex and error-prone when configured manually.
rows.exitCriteria: `protheus connector add telegram` runs guided multi-topic setup with concurrency-safe defaults and receipt-linked provisioning state.

rows.upgrade: In-Browser File Browser/Editor + Git View
rows.why: File-level operations still require shell workflows, limiting accessibility for non-terminal operators.
rows.exitCriteria: GUI supports browse/edit/commit workflows with explicit git state visibility and deterministic file-operation receipts.

rows.upgrade: Watchdog Self-Healing + Webhook Replay/Debug + Token Analytics
rows.why: Runtime failures and webhook/debug telemetry handling are fragmented, making incident response slower and less predictable.
rows.exitCriteria: Add always-on watchdog restart policies, webhook replay/debug tooling, token analytics view, and one-click update controls with deterministic operation receipts.

rows.upgrade: Conduit-Only Management Enforcement + Receipts
rows.why: GUI/ops actions can bypass canonical policy and audit paths when executed via direct management surfaces.
rows.exitCriteria: Route all GUI management, backup, drift, webhook, key-management, and watchdog actions through conduit with capability/policy checks and deterministic receipts; bypass paths fail closed by contract checks.

rows.upgrade: Creator Discovery Engine (YouTube/Twitch)
rows.why: Creator lead generation is manual and inconsistent, limiting campaign scale and response speed.
rows.exitCriteria: `protheus app run creator-outreach` discovers and scores creators by niche/engagement/fit (100+ target per run) with deterministic discovery receipts.
rows.section: Creator Outreach Automation App Intake (Source: OpenClaw creator-outreach demo pattern, 2026-03-09)

rows.upgrade: Personalized Outreach + Follow-Up Sequences
rows.why: Outreach messaging lacks individualized personalization and repeatable follow-up automation.
rows.exitCriteria: Generate unique per-creator messages and follow-up sequences via approved connectors with receipt-linked prompt/template metadata and send outcomes.
rows.section: Creator Outreach Automation App Intake (Source: OpenClaw creator-outreach demo pattern, 2026-03-09)

rows.upgrade: Response Handling + Call Booking Workflow
rows.why: Reply handling and meeting booking are fragmented and can miss qualified responses in high-volume campaigns.
rows.exitCriteria: Run 24/7 response triage + booking flow with human-review gate options, deterministic response receipts, and booking event linkage.
rows.section: Creator Outreach Automation App Intake (Source: OpenClaw creator-outreach demo pattern, 2026-03-09)

rows.upgrade: 24/7 Autonomous Campaign Operation Profile
rows.why: Campaign execution stalls without continuous schedule/heartbeat operation when users are offline.
rows.exitCriteria: Support persistent scheduled/background operation with campaign state checkpoints, health markers, and resumable execution receipts.
rows.section: Creator Outreach Automation App Intake (Source: OpenClaw creator-outreach demo pattern, 2026-03-09)

rows.upgrade: Conduit-Only Outreach Enforcement + Default `/apps` Placement
rows.why: Outreach apps can bypass policy controls or drift into client-owned authority paths without explicit placement and boundary contracts.
rows.exitCriteria: Ship scaffold at `/apps/creator_outreach/`, keep client wrappers thin, and enforce conduit-only routing for discovery/sending/response/booking with deterministic receipts and policy checks.
rows.section: Creator Outreach Automation App Intake (Source: OpenClaw creator-outreach demo pattern, 2026-03-09)

rows.upgrade: Micro-Dopamine Event Engine + Objective Auto-Verification
rows.why: Motivation/reward feedback flatlines when only manually verified achievements count, leaving obvious maintenance/progress work unrewarded.
rows.exitCriteria: Add deterministic micro-event registry (issue creation, health checks, anomaly logging, docs, community maintenance, etc.) with capped point schedules and auto-verification for objective work classes; all accrual writes receipt-linked dopamine ledger entries and exposes weekly roll-up state without requiring human confirmation for routine objective work.

rows.upgrade: Confidence-Gated Autophagy Auto-Approval + Rollback Window
rows.why: Self-optimization proposals stall for days because every proposal waits on manual approval even when confidence and rollback posture are strong.
rows.exitCriteria: Add auto-approval policy for bounded proposal classes with configurable thresholds (`min_confidence`, historical success floor, max impact score, exclusions), supervised delayed-commit flow, and automatic rollback/regret-issue creation on degradation; high-confidence proposals execute within a bounded rollback window and emit approval/rollback receipts.

rows.upgrade: Progressive Right-Hemisphere Task Classifier + Synthesis Windows
rows.why: Dual-brain remains stuck in left-only mode because no task classifier safely distinguishes creative/synthetic work from execution-critical work.
rows.exitCriteria: Add task-class policy for right-brain eligibility (`synthesis`, `creative_writing`, `pattern_recognition`, `strategy`, `design`, `meta_analysis`) with safe zones, rollback triggers, and bounded scheduled synthesis sessions; right-brain usage becomes measurable and fail-closed for execution/health/alert tasks.

rows.upgrade: Async Health Monitoring Envelope
rows.why: Blocking health checks and immediate failure escalation increase cognitive overhead and make the system feel heavier than necessary.
rows.exitCriteria: Move health polling into background async envelopes with non-blocking execution, configurable escalation after consecutive failures, and aggregated hourly health receipts; normal operation is not blocked on routine health checks unless explicit critical policy says so.

rows.upgrade: Tiered Verbosity + Deep-Dive Instrumentation Mode
rows.why: Every action currently risks producing full-strength monitoring artifacts, creating noise and operator fatigue.
rows.exitCriteria: Add severity-aware instrumentation policy (`critical/full`, `normal/lightweight`, `routine/sampled`, `noise/suppressed`) plus time-bounded deep-dive mode that temporarily restores full capture for debugging/anomaly investigation; routine instrumentation noise drops while preserving critical auditability.

rows.upgrade: Unified Daily Standup + Critical Update Bridge
rows.why: The operator experience is fragmented across health checks, proposals, blockers, and activity surfaces with no single daily briefing.
rows.exitCriteria: Auto-generate a daily standup summary (`yesterday`, `today`, `blockers`, `proposed actions`) from receipts/state and publish it to the governed operator surfaces (Linear/Slack/internal dashboard as policy allows); critical escalations can mirror into the same channel family with deterministic delivery receipts.

rows.upgrade: Weekly Cohort Dopamine Scoring + Trend View
rows.why: Daily scoring creates pressure and poor trend visibility, so dopamine feedback is unstable and hard to interpret.
rows.exitCriteria: Add weekly cohort scoring model with targets/bonuses, trend summaries, and roll-up reporting tied to the dopamine ledger so operators can review directional progress instead of noisy daily fluctuations.

rows.upgrade: Chapter-by-Chapter Generation Profile
rows.why: Long-form writing sessions drift in tone and plot without a governed chapter generation loop tied to persistent state.
rows.exitCriteria: `protheus app run novel "<prompt>"` generates structured chapter outputs that bind to existing persona + memory primitives and emit deterministic generation receipts per chapter.

rows.upgrade: Character/World Consistency Contract over Epistemic Objects
rows.why: Character sheets, lore, and relationship state become inconsistent when not enforced through one persistent object contract.
rows.exitCriteria: Chapter generation reads/writes character/world/plot objects through existing memory primitives; consistency checks run per chapter and emit deterministic validation receipts with any detected drift markers.

rows.upgrade: Streaming Editor + Chapter Navigation Reference App (`/apps/novel`)
rows.why: Creative workflows are slow without live streaming output and chapter-level edit/navigation controls in one focused app surface.
rows.exitCriteria: Ship `/apps/novel/` with streaming markdown editor, chapter navigation, and revision actions as thin app UX over existing streaming/edit primitives; no duplicate orchestration authority in client code.

rows.upgrade: Multi-Model Plot/Prose Routing Profile
rows.why: Plot planning and prose drafting need different model characteristics, but current usage is manually selected and inconsistent.
rows.exitCriteria: Use existing Layer-2 routing primitives to split planning/prose tasks by policy-scoped profiles with deterministic routing decision receipts and budget telemetry.

rows.upgrade: Conduit-Only Creative Workflow Enforcement + `/apps` Placement Contract
rows.why: Story generation/edit flows can bypass policy and boundary rules when embedded directly into client/runtime authority lanes.
rows.exitCriteria: Keep implementation in `/apps/novel/`, keep client wrappers thin (`run/status/edit/view`), and route generate/revise/export actions through conduit with fail-closed policy checks and deterministic receipts.

rows.upgrade: Side-by-Side Multi-Model Streaming Compare Session Contract
rows.why: Model selection quality is weak without synchronized, same-prompt comparisons across multiple local models under one deterministic session contract.
rows.exitCriteria: `protheus compare models <a> <b> [c] [d]` runs a bounded compare session (2-4 models policy-scoped) with synchronized streaming output lanes and deterministic per-model/per-step receipts linked to one session id.
rows.section: Local LLM Comparison & Multi-Model Streaming Intake (Doc `1aJJ7LhHU1zJWlngBR76qEdQ02Ey1zrPtxBu6TZ_Pioc`, 2026-03-11)

rows.upgrade: Live Per-Model Performance Metrics Pipeline
rows.why: Developers cannot reliably evaluate throughput/latency tradeoffs when metrics are sampled inconsistently or detached from generation sessions.
rows.exitCriteria: Compare sessions surface real-time per-model metrics (tokens/sec, first-token latency, end-to-end latency, memory footprint) with deterministic metric receipts and session-linked observability output.
rows.section: Local LLM Comparison & Multi-Model Streaming Intake (Doc `1aJJ7LhHU1zJWlngBR76qEdQ02Ey1zrPtxBu6TZ_Pioc`, 2026-03-11)

rows.upgrade: Governed Local Model Discovery + Hot-Swap Contract
rows.why: Local-model workflows are brittle when model inventory, quantization fit, and hot-swap behavior are not standardized under one contract.
rows.exitCriteria: Provide governed local-model discovery and policy-bounded swap/load flows (including quantization/memory-fit checks) so compare sessions can switch models without losing audit lineage; every load/swap decision emits deterministic receipts.
rows.section: Local LLM Comparison & Multi-Model Streaming Intake (Doc `1aJJ7LhHU1zJWlngBR76qEdQ02Ey1zrPtxBu6TZ_Pioc`, 2026-03-11)

rows.upgrade: Conduit-Only Compare Execution + Receipts
rows.why: Parallel local inference calls can bypass policy/budget controls when driven directly from UI/runtime adapters.
rows.exitCriteria: Route all compare-session actions (fanout, stream, metrics, load/swap, stop/export) through conduit with fail-closed policy checks, deterministic receipts, and contract tests that reject bypass paths.
rows.section: Local LLM Comparison & Multi-Model Streaming Intake (Doc `1aJJ7LhHU1zJWlngBR76qEdQ02Ey1zrPtxBu6TZ_Pioc`, 2026-03-11)

rows.upgrade: Persona/Shadow Compare Profile + Thin UX Surface
rows.why: Research/testing personas need reusable comparison mode without embedding inference authority into client interfaces.
rows.exitCriteria: Add optional research/testing persona profile plus thin CLI/observability surface (`protheus compare`) over core compare-session contracts; no duplicate orchestration logic in client code.
rows.section: Local LLM Comparison & Multi-Model Streaming Intake (Doc `1aJJ7LhHU1zJWlngBR76qEdQ02Ey1zrPtxBu6TZ_Pioc`, 2026-03-11)

rows.upgrade: Automated Hallucination/Faithfulness/Answer-Relevance Scoring
rows.why: Agent quality issues are discovered late because core runs lack consistent post-response evaluation signals.
rows.exitCriteria: Add bounded automated scoring lanes for hallucination risk, faithfulness to supplied context, and answer relevance on governed run classes; each evaluated response emits deterministic score receipts linked to run/session ids.

rows.upgrade: RAG Retrieval/Context/Citation Relevance Evaluators
rows.why: Retrieval workflows degrade silently when retrieval quality and citation grounding are not continuously measured.
rows.exitCriteria: Add RAG-oriented evaluators (retrieval quality, context relevance, citation grounding/accuracy) that store results as Epistemic Objects with provenance/confidence labels and deterministic evaluation receipts.

rows.upgrade: Custom Metric Registration Contract (Template + Policy)
rows.why: Teams need domain-specific quality metrics, but ad hoc evaluators create inconsistent execution and safety posture.
rows.exitCriteria: Provide governed custom metric registration (schema/template-driven) with policy-scoped execution limits and deterministic registration/invocation receipts; registered metrics can be attached to selected workflows without bypassing conduit checks.

rows.upgrade: Structured Reporting + Trend Dashboard Contract
rows.why: Raw point-in-time scores are hard to operationalize without longitudinal views, pass/fail summaries, and failure exemplars.
rows.exitCriteria: Generate structured evaluation reports (pass/fail, trend slices, representative failures, metric drift) available via CLI/dashboard surfaces, all linked to deterministic report-generation receipts.

rows.upgrade: Conduit-Only Evaluation Enforcement + Thin Client Boundary
rows.why: Evaluation systems can become unaudited side channels if scoring/reporting paths run outside canonical policy and receipt flow.
rows.exitCriteria: Enforce conduit-only routing for evaluation, metric registration, and report generation with fail-closed policy checks; keep client as thin display/control surfaces with no evaluation authority logic embedded.

rows.upgrade: Pre-LLM Context Assembly Trace Contract
rows.why: Operators cannot reliably audit model behavior when the exact assembled context payload is opaque before invocation.
rows.exitCriteria: Add deterministic pre-call context trace artifact (`system`, `history`, `tool schemas`, `skills`, `memory`) attached to each LLM call receipt; support `protheus trace context` for on-demand replay without exposing disallowed sensitive fields.
rows.section: Opik Native Observability Source Coverage Intake (Doc `1kl5o9PRvp98-6Z6oYnp1feviefzJSGrELDf0sq8fIR4`, 2026-03-11)

rows.upgrade: Native Gateway Observability Plugin Contract
rows.why: External proxy tracing misses runtime-internal events and creates governance bypass risk for sensitive traces.
rows.exitCriteria: Add in-gateway plugin host contract for observability plugins with signed manifests, capability-scoped access, isolation policy, and deterministic plugin lifecycle receipts (`install`, `activate`, `suspend`, `remove`).
rows.section: Opik Native Observability Source Coverage Intake (Doc `1kl5o9PRvp98-6Z6oYnp1feviefzJSGrELDf0sq8fIR4`, 2026-03-11)

rows.upgrade: Tool/Delegation End-to-End Lineage Correlation Contract
rows.why: Tool inputs/outputs/errors and sub-agent handoffs are hard to debug when events are not causally stitched to parent requests.
rows.exitCriteria: Correlate every tool call and delegation hop into one lineage graph linked to parent request/session ids, with deterministic timing/error/state receipts and drill-down queries in observability surfaces.
rows.section: Opik Native Observability Source Coverage Intake (Doc `1kl5o9PRvp98-6Z6oYnp1feviefzJSGrELDf0sq8fIR4`, 2026-03-11)

rows.upgrade: Per-Request Model + Cost + Thread Ledger Contract
rows.why: Cost visibility is fragmented when usage is aggregated only at coarse time windows without request-level model/provider attribution.
rows.exitCriteria: Emit request-level cost ledger entries (model, provider, tokens, unit costs, totals, thread/session linkage) with exportable historical trend views and deterministic accounting receipts.
rows.section: Opik Native Observability Source Coverage Intake (Doc `1kl5o9PRvp98-6Z6oYnp1feviefzJSGrELDf0sq8fIR4`, 2026-03-11)

rows.upgrade: Curated Native Observability Plugin Template Governance Lane
rows.why: Plugin quality and safety drift without signed curated templates and periodic review of tracing patterns/privacy controls.
rows.exitCriteria: Maintain signed plugin template pack installable via governed URI (for example `observability://opik-native`) with quarterly human review, compatibility metadata, and deterministic install/update/deprecate receipts.
rows.section: Opik Native Observability Source Coverage Intake (Doc `1kl5o9PRvp98-6Z6oYnp1feviefzJSGrELDf0sq8fIR4`, 2026-03-11)

rows.upgrade: Conduit-Only Deep-Trace Boundary + Thin Client Enforcement
rows.why: Deep-tracing features can leak authority into client code paths unless plugin/tracing control is centrally enforced.
rows.exitCriteria: Enforce conduit-only routing for trace capture, plugin control, lineage queries, and ledger exports with fail-closed policy checks and bypass-rejection tests; client/app remain non-authoritative render/control surfaces.
rows.section: Opik Native Observability Source Coverage Intake (Doc `1kl5o9PRvp98-6Z6oYnp1feviefzJSGrELDf0sq8fIR4`, 2026-03-11)

rows.upgrade: Local Commandment Rule Objects + Priority/Conflict Contract
rows.why: Users need plain-language local rules ("never do X without confirmation"), but ad hoc prompt-only rules are brittle and non-auditable across long-running sessions.
rows.exitCriteria: Add governed commandment-rule objects (scope, action class, confirm/block mode, priority, conflict policy) evaluated in conduit policy flow; all rule evaluations emit deterministic allow/deny/override receipts.
rows.section: Local Policy Enforcement & Hash-Chained Audit Trail Source Coverage Addendum (Doc `1jXTbAvxCT7NgxuGi4620HJT0zrfqW8mfYJE4jjDzP5s`, 2026-03-11)

rows.upgrade: Violation-Linked Hash-Chain Audit Continuity Contract
rows.why: Existing receipt chains lose policy intent visibility when violation events are not first-class linked artifacts.
rows.exitCriteria: Extend receipt lineage so commandment checks and violation decisions are appended as hash-linked events with actor/tool/action context and immutable replay continuity in `protheus audit`/`protheus-top`.
rows.section: Local Policy Enforcement & Hash-Chained Audit Trail Source Coverage Addendum (Doc `1jXTbAvxCT7NgxuGi4620HJT0zrfqW8mfYJE4jjDzP5s`, 2026-03-11)

rows.upgrade: Enforcement-Focused Live Monitor + Notification Contract
rows.why: Operators cannot react quickly when policy blocks/flags are buried inside generic telemetry streams.

rows.upgrade: Melt/Refine Iteration + Regression Gate Contract
rows.why: Parallel drops degrade quality unless every cycle includes deterministic refine/retest loops and full regression gates.
rows.exitCriteria: Execute automated melt/refine passes after each drop batch, require configured regression suites before promotion, and emit deterministic pass/fail/rollback receipts per iteration.
rows.section: Autonomous Snowball Orchestration Engine Source Coverage Intake (Doc `1OA9Hu3dx9iheyIW-7JGrR4hd8bVJt0TE21Bv_fRNeFg`, 2026-03-11)

rows.upgrade: Compaction + Sphere-of-Ice Snapshot Contract
rows.why: Continuous change creates context and artifact sprawl without explicit compaction/snapshot boundaries per cycle.
rows.exitCriteria: After successful cycle gates, compact artifacts/context into versioned cycle snapshots with deterministic provenance hashes and reversible restore pointers.
rows.section: Autonomous Snowball Orchestration Engine Source Coverage Intake (Doc `1OA9Hu3dx9iheyIW-7JGrR4hd8bVJt0TE21Bv_fRNeFg`, 2026-03-11)

rows.upgrade: Next-Snow Backlog Packing Contract
rows.why: Improvement momentum stalls when next-cycle backlog assembly is manual and disconnected from cycle evidence.
rows.exitCriteria: Auto-generate next snow backlog from unresolved findings/opportunities with dependency ordering, confidence scores, and deterministic backlog-pack receipts.
rows.section: Autonomous Snowball Orchestration Engine Source Coverage Intake (Doc `1OA9Hu3dx9iheyIW-7JGrR4hd8bVJt0TE21Bv_fRNeFg`, 2026-03-11)

rows.upgrade: Live Cycle Visibility + Operator Control Contract (`protheus snowball status` / `protheus snowball compact`)
rows.why: Operators need cycle transparency and intervention controls without embedding orchestration authority in client code.
rows.exitCriteria: Provide thin status/control surfaces showing current cycle stage, batch outcomes, regression state, and compact/backlog controls backed entirely by conduit-routed app runtime actions.
rows.section: Autonomous Snowball Orchestration Engine Source Coverage Intake (Doc `1OA9Hu3dx9iheyIW-7JGrR4hd8bVJt0TE21Bv_fRNeFg`, 2026-03-11)

rows.upgrade: Conduit-Only Snowball Boundary + App-Layer Placement Enforcement
rows.why: Self-management orchestration can bypass policy if implemented directly as client-side scripts.
rows.exitCriteria: Enforce app-layer placement (`/apps/snowball_engine/`) and conduit-only routing for start/melt/compact/regress/pack actions; boundary tests reject client-owned authority paths.
rows.section: Autonomous Snowball Orchestration Engine Source Coverage Intake (Doc `1OA9Hu3dx9iheyIW-7JGrR4hd8bVJt0TE21Bv_fRNeFg`, 2026-03-11)

rows.upgrade: External Semantic Skill Catalog Sync Contract
rows.why: Skill discovery remains fragmented when large external catalogs are not mirrored/indexed under one governed sync path.
rows.exitCriteria: Add governed sync/mirror contract for external skill directories with deterministic ingest receipts, signature/provenance metadata capture, and fail-closed handling for malformed or untrusted entries.

rows.upgrade: Natural-Language Skill Discovery + Faceted Ranking Contract
rows.why: Users cannot efficiently find relevant skills without semantic search plus stable popular/recent/tag ranking views.
rows.exitCriteria: Extend skill search with semantic retrieval and deterministic ranking facets (`relevance`, `popular`, `recent`, `tags`) backed by receipted index metadata and policy-bounded query scopes.

rows.upgrade: One-Click Install + Dependency Resolver Hardening for External Skills
rows.why: External one-click installs are brittle without deterministic dependency/compatibility resolution and activation gates.
rows.exitCriteria: Add install resolver that validates dependency trees, runner compatibility, trust policy, and activation prerequisites before install; emits deterministic install/deny receipts with remediation hints.

rows.why: Installed skills are underused when persona routing cannot suggest or bind relevant skills safely at runtime.
